verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (Hsp70/HSP72) is primarily a cytosolic chaperone, but there is well-documented cell-state-induced surface translocation: under heat shock, oxidative stress, and in tumor cells, Hsp70 is trafficked to and stably displayed on the outer plasma membrane leaflet. Surface Hsp70 on tumor cells is a recognized target for NK cell-mediated killing and has been exploited in therapeutic antibody and CAR-NK programs engaging surface-exposed Hsp70 on cancer cells. This surface exposure is cell-state-dependent (stress/tumor context) rather than constitutive, warranting 'contextual' rather than 'yes'.
